items.title: Heat wave smashes records across central US
items.summary: WASHINGTON: A record-breaking heat wave baked the central United States on Sunday, smashing temperature records from the northern plains to the Rocky Mountains region. Both temperatures are all-time highs for each city since records started being kept over 150 years ago, surpassing the previous records of 42C. Just over a week ago, the eastern United States was gripped by another heat wave that pushed temperatures to around 40C in New York and Philadelphia.

items.title: Zahid to meet Nga over resignation remarks after BN's Johor win
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said he will meet Housing and Local Government Minister Nga Kor Ming after the latter's pledge to resign if Barisan Nasional (BN) secured a major victory in the Johor election. Zahid, who is also Umno president, said such remarks might be expected from the opposition but were unnecessary among parties serving in the same unity government. Asked about federal-level ties following BN's victory in the Johor election, Zahid said relations within the unity government remained very good.

items.title: EU countries weigh trade ban on Israeli settlements
items.summary: BRUSSELS: EU foreign ministers on Monday discussed imposing an import ban on products from Israeli settlements, after pressure from a raft of member states. "Everybody agrees that the situation in the West Bank is really intolerable," EU foreign policy chief Kaja Kallas said at the start of a meeting in Brussels. Several EU countries – including Ireland, the Netherlands, and Spain – have already imposed their own trade restrictions on Israeli settlements in the occupied Palestinian territories, considered illegal under international law.

items.title: Zahid: BN's Negri Sembilan strategy tailored to local landscape
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Barisan Nasional (BN) will adopt a formula tailored to Negri Sembilan's political landscape when determining its candidates for the state election. BN chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said the approach would not focus solely on candidates, but also take into account factors unique to the state. Yesterday, BN secretary-general Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abdul Kadir said the coalition would apply the election "formula" used in its recent landslide victory in Johor to the upcoming Negri Sembilan polls.

items.title: Unicef, NUTP, academics and NGOs all in: Education Ministry goes all-hands-on-deck to tackle student safety crisis
items.summary: IPOH, July 13 — The Ministry of Education (MOE) is strengthening efforts to safeguard students’ mental well-being and enhance school safety through a series of preventive and support initiatives. Deputy Education Minister Wong Kah Woh said the measures include the establishment of the Special Committee on Educational Institution Safety Reform, comprising stakeholders from within the ministry, academic experts, government agencies and non-governmental organisations (NGOs). He said the ministry treated every incident involving students with the utmost seriousness and had instructed all State Education Departments (JPN) and District Education Offices (PPD) to strictly follow existing guidelines and report cases immediately.

items.title: 'No agreement with Pas': Zahid dismisses seat talks claim
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Umno has no agreement with Pas on seat negotiations for the Negri Sembilan election, says Umno president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi. Responding to Pas president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang's claim that talks between the two parties were ongoing, Zahid said any discussions were merely based on an understanding and had yet to result in a final agreement. He said not all statements from Pas could be taken as accurate, describing Hadi's remarks as a political statement by the party.

items.title: ‘I could have bowed’: Syed Saddiq says six-year ordeal proves principle outweighs fleeting power
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, July 13 — Syed Saddiq Syed Abdul Rahman walked out of the Federal Court today with his acquittal intact — declaring the fight was about principle, not just personal freedom. “Malaysia must be led by those willing to lose position for principle, not sell principle for position. I could have stayed silent.

items.title: ‘Cheerful, sociable, active, loving’: In inquest, psychologist says Zara Qairina’s profile challenges suicide theory
items.summary: Child Psychologist Dr Noor Aishah Rosli, 51, who is also a registered counsellor, said the finding was based on an overall assessment of Zara Qairina’s development from birth until she was 13. The 76th witness said the psychological autopsy approach was used to prepare Zara Qairina’s profile, which involved a retrospective forensic psychology assessment of her mental, emotional, social, behavioural, interpersonal and environmental state before her death. “I also evaluated whether there were psychological, social, family, school environment, peer relationship, hostel experience, personal writings, phone communications and witness information factors that could help the Court understand the full context of Zara’s life before the incident,” she said.

items.title: 'We can finally prepare for the wedding,' says Syed Saddiq's mother
items.summary: https://www.bharian.com.my/berita/nasional/2026/07/1587252/kami-akan-buat-persiapan-kahwin-syed-saddiq-dan-bella-biarlah⁠ PUTRAJAYA: Wedding preparations for Syed Saddiq Syed Abdul Rahman and singer-actress Bella Astillah can finally begin after the Federal Court ruled in his favour today. His mother, Sharifah Mahani Syed Abdul Aziz, said the family had put their wedding plans on hold until the court delivered its decision. "We really couldn't make any preparations because we were waiting for today's decision.

items.title: Bangladeshi man breaks down as Penang raids net 21 undocumented migrants
items.summary: Penang Immigration director Syamsul Fitry Ahmad said the operation, which began at 9am, targeted premises suspected of employing or being operated by foreigners based on public complaints and intelligence. "The operation targeted business premises based on complaints and intelligence gathered. We checked four premises and detained 21 foreigners for various immigration offences," he said after the operation at Jalan Datuk Keramat here today.

items.title: [UPDATED] Immigration mulls higher fines for hiring undocumented workers
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Immigration Department may propose higher fines for employers who hire undocumented foreign workers if the current maximum compound of RM10,000 per worker fails to act as a deterrent. Immigration director-general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban said many employers remained undeterred and continued to hire undocumented migrants despite continuous nationwide enforcement. Zakaria said employers often cite the high cost of legal recruitment, including processing fees, transportation and other expenses, as the reason they turn to undocumented migrants.

items.title: SD Property loses bid for extra WCE land compensation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Sime Darby Property Bhd has lost its court bid to retain an additional RM78.6 million in compensation arising from the government's acquisition of its land for the West Coast Expressway (WCE) project. In a Bursa Malaysia filing on Monday, SD Property said the Shah Alam High Court ruled in favour of the Malaysian Highway Authority (LLM), setting aside the RM78.6 million awarded to its wholly owned unit, Sime Darby Property (Klang) Sdn Bhd, for severance and injurious affection. In 2015, the Klang district land administrator awarded total compensation of RM169.3 million, comprising RM90.7 million for the land's market value and RM78.6 million for severance and injurious affection.

items.title: [UPDATED] 111 undocumented migrants nabbed in raid on Jalan Kenanga shops
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Immigration officers swept through Jalan Kenanga's wholesale clothing hub today, arresting 111 undocumented migrants during a raid. The raids sparked chaotic scenes as undocumented migrants tried to escape through back lanes and adjoining buildings after spotting the officers. [[nid:1487675]] In another shop, enforcement personnel used grinders to cut through locked metal doors before uncovering several migrants hiding beneath piles of boxes and inside storage areas.

items.title: Banks embrace AI, but trust gap persists: Study
items.summary: However, confidence in AI remains limited, with only 25 per cent of respondents saying they trust AI-generated outputs enough to support key business decisions. Only 15 per cent have reached an established level of AI maturity, while just 2 per cent are considered advanced, where AI is fully embedded into decision-making and business strategy. Only 26 per cent of institutions have a clearly defined AI strategy linked to business objectives, despite 44 per cent already developing customised AI solutions.

items.title: Ops Mega nets 503 undocumented foreigners in nationwide raids
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Immigration Department detained 503 undocumented foreigners in simultaneous raids at 16 locations nationwide under a two-day Ops Mega enforcement operation. The operation targeted businesses suspected of employing undocumented migrants and those allegedly operated by foreigners. Immigration director-general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban said the nationwide operation, which began yesterday and continued today, involved 876 enforcement personnel from the Immigration Department, National Registration Department (NRD), Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry (KPDN), local authorities and police.

items.title: Prosecution accepts Federal Court's decision to uphold Syed Saddiq's acquittal
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The prosecution in the case involving Muar member of parliament Syed Saddiq Syed Abdul Rahman said it respected the Federal Court's decision to uphold his acquittal and discharge on corruption-related charges. Deputy public prosecutor Datuk Wan Shaharuddin Wan Ladin said the prosecution was grateful for the court's decision. "We will wait for the Attorney-General's decision," Wan Shaharuddin said.

items.title: Sultan Sharafuddin takes LRT3 ride, honours 13,000 workers
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Sultan of Selangor, Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah, took a ride on the Shah Alam LRT3 Line today from Bandar Utama to Johan Setia, accompanied by Tengku Permaisuri Selangor and the Raja Muda of Selangor. In a statement from Istana Alam Shah, the ruler congratulated Prasarana Malaysia Bhd on completing the long-awaited line, saying it would help ease congestion and improve public transport connectivity in Shah Alam, Klang and surrounding areas. "Alhamdulillah, the Shah Alam LRT3 Line is now complete and can be used.

items.title: PH to unveil Negri Sembilan election line-up tomorrow night
items.summary: SEREMBAN: Pakatan Harapan (PH) will announce its full list of candidates for the Negri Sembilan state election tomorrow night. According to a post on PH's official Facebook page, the announcement will be made by PH chairman Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim at the PH Negri Sembilan Candidates Announcement Ceremony at Dataran Melang, Kuala Pilah. The event is expected to be attended by PH deputy chairmen Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu and Anthony Loke, as well as PH Negri Sembilan chairman Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun.

items.title: FOI Bill to be referred to special select committee for 'comprehensive review'
items.summary: Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Law and Institutional Reform) Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said said this step would allow for a more comprehensive review of every clause through engagement with members of parliament from both sides and various stakeholders. In a statement, Azalina said the move reflected the government's approach to implementing major and complex institutional reforms by shaping balanced and publicly trusted legal frameworks. Azalina said the bill represented an important step in strengthening transparency, accountability, and good governance, while improving public access to official government information in an orderly and legally grounded manner.

items.title: Deputy finance minister pitches one-stop tech financing hub in Penang-Kulim area to power Malaysia’s true ‘Silicon Valley’ dream
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 13 — Deputy Finance Minister Liew Chin Tong has proposed that Malaysia establish a one-stop centre for semiconductor and deep-tech financing, offering integrated financing solutions in the heart of the country’s technology cluster. He said the move would help turn the technology cluster into Malaysia’s true Silicon Valley by bridging the gap between deep tech and capital. Liew said the one-stop centre could support themes, and not only single names, inviting investors to back a well-governed national story built on trust in Malaysia’s regulatory framework.

items.title: Sungai Perak R&R to close temporarily for monkey control operation
items.summary: IPOH: The Sungai Perak Rest and Service Area (R&R) northbound will be temporarily closed tomorrow for an operation to address wild monkey disturbances. PLUS said the closure, including the R&R petrol station, was necessary to ensure the safety of highway users, personnel involved and the smooth execution of the operation. "Throughout the operation, the Sungai Perak R&R (northbound), including the petrol station, will be temporarily closed," it said in a statement on social media.

items.title: Global shares skid as Gulf conflict sends oil surging
items.summary: Brent crude climbed 4.3 per cent to reach US$79.31 a barrel, up from the recent trough of US$70.14, while U.S. crude added 4.4 per cent to US$74.62 a barrel. S&P 500 futures dipped 0.6 per cent while Nasdaq futures lost 1.3 per cent. In Europe, EUROSTOXX 50 futures fell 0.9 per cent, while DAX futures declined 1.0 per cent and FTSE futures eased 0.3 per cent.

items.title: Selangor Sultan rides the LRT3 train, says Shah Alam Line should have been completed earlier, but delay had its ‘wisdom’
items.summary: KLANG, July 13 — The Sultan of Selangor today said the Shah Alam LRT3 line should have been completed according to its original schedule, although His Royal Highness added that he believed there was “wisdom” behind the delay. The remarks came in a statement from the Selangor Royal Office issued after Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah travelled on the newly opened line from Bandar Utama station in Petaling Jaya to Johan Setia station in Klang. “It would have been preferable if the LRT3 line had been completed according to its original schedule, as I view the rail service as an essential public transport facility for the convenience of the people.” Sultan Sharafuddin said he was nevertheless grateful the long-awaited rail line had finally become a reality, describing it as a project that would improve the quality of life, ease traffic congestion and provide a more efficient, comfortable and sustainable public transport system.

items.title: Kenanga Research keeps 2.9pct jobless forecast, upgrades 2026 GDP outlook
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Kenanga Research has maintained its 2026 unemployment rate forecast at 2.9 per cent while raising its gross domestic product (GDP) growth forecast to 4.5-5.0 per cent. The firm cited resilient labour market conditions and strengthening domestic demand despite external uncertainties. Malaysia's unemployment rate remained unchanged at 3.0 per cent in May, while employment expanded 0.1 per cent month-on-month for the fourth consecutive month.

items.title: Shipping recovery to take time as Malaysian ports prepare for Hormuz backlog
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Ports, shipping lines and cargo owners are now confronting the formidable task of restoring global supply chains and clearing a backlog of vessels and cargo built up during months of conflict in and around the Strait of Hormuz, a process that industry players say could take weeks or even months. For Malaysia, the gradual restoration of shipping activity between Asia, the Middle East and Europe could translate into higher vessel traffic and cargo movements through ports along the Strait of Melaka, one of the world's busiest maritime trade routes representing nearly 22 per cent of global trade and 29 per cent of maritime oil. Three-Phase Recovery Expected Federation of Malaysian Freight Forwarders (FMFF) president Datuk Dr Tony Chia Han Teun said the sailing of the vessels through Hormuz would likely be done through three phases, whereby energy cargo would be prioritised before gradually restoring container and bulk shipping networks over the coming weeks and months.

items.title: What’s next for the internet? MCMC gathers global minds in KL for digital future talks from July 21
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 13 — The Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) will host the International Regulatory Conference (IRC) 2026 on July 21 and 22, offering a global platform for dialogue on the next digital era while reinforcing Malaysia’s voice in international regulatory conversations. Held under the theme “Shaping the Next Digital Era: Regulation, Resilience and Trust,” the two-day event at the Shangri-La Kuala Lumpur will gather regulators, industry leaders and experts to explore digital economy and regulatory issues facing the communications and multimedia sector. Expected to be launched by Communications Minister Datuk Seri Fahmi Fadzil, the conference aims to establish Malaysia as a leader in global telecommunications policy and enhance MCMC’s engagement in international regulatory affairs, the commission said.

items.title: Judge BN on its track record, not politics of hate, says Rauf
items.summary: MELAKA: Chief Minister Datuk Seri Ab Rauf Yusoh has urged voters to judge Barisan Nasional (BN) based on its track record in developing the state and reject politics driven by hatred and provocation. Ab Rauf, who is also Melaka BN chairman, said the people should assess the coalition's performance rather than be swayed by slander or divisive rhetoric. The current term of the Melaka State Legislative Assembly expires at the end of December.

items.title: Tabung Haji shuts door on special Haj fast lane for retirees, sticks with ‘first-registered, first-served’ rule
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 13 — Tabung Haji (TH) will continue to implement its Haj offer system based on the “first-registered, first-served” principle, without introducing special categories, to ensure all depositors are given a fair and equitable opportunity in line with the quota allocated by the Saudi Arabian government. Deputy Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Religious Affairs) Marhamah Rosli said introducing special Haj offer categories could disrupt the existing queue system and disadvantage depositors who have waited years for their turn to perform the pilgrimage. Responding to a supplementary question from Abdul Latiff on efforts to secure additional Haj quotas, Marhamah said Malaysia was allocated an official quota of 31,600 pilgrims for this year’s Haj season, while any additional quota remains the sole prerogative of the Saudi Arabian government.

items.title: Hormuz traffic slows to multi-week low as renewed US, Iran strikes raise safety risk
items.summary: SINGAPORE: The number of vessels transiting the Strait of Hormuz fell to multi-week lows on Sunday, shipping data showed, as renewed strikes between the US and Iran and attacks on ships in the Middle East heightened safety concerns. Six vessels transited the strait on Sunday, ship-tracking data from Kpler showed, the lowest number in five weeks. One tanker controlled by the Abu Dhabi National Oil Co exited the strait between July 10 and July 12, Kpler data showed.

items.title: Singapore president gets 21-gun salute at Istana Negara in first Malaysia state visit
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 13 — Singaporean President Tharman Shanmugaratnam was accorded a state welcoming ceremony at Istana Negara today, marking his first state visit to Malaysia since assuming office in September 2023. President Tharman and his wife, Jane Ittogi Shanmugaratnam, arrived at Istana Negara at 10am and were received by His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, and Her Majesty Raja Zarith Sofiah, Queen of Malaysia. Also in attendance were Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim and his wife, Datuk Seri Dr Wan Azizah Wan Ismail; Deputy Prime Ministers Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi and Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof; as well as Cabinet ministers.

items.title: Psychologist tells Zara inquest child assessment should begin from the womb
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: A child psychologist told the Coroner's Court today that a person's background should be assessed from the time they were in the womb, as an expectant mother's emotional state could influence a child's psychological development. Dr Noor Aishah Rosli, 51, said studies showed that severe maternal depression during pregnancy could have different effects depending on the child's sex. "But if a mother who is severely depressed is carrying a baby boy, the child is more likely to become aggressive," she told coroner Amir Shah Amir Hassan on the 79th day of the inquest into the death of Zara Qairina Mahathir.

items.title: Football critic assaulted, suffers fractured eye socket [WATCH]
items.summary: PETALING JAYA: A YouTuber and football critic in his 40s suffered a fractured eye socket and other injuries after he was assaulted by two unidentified men at a petrol station near Bandar Sri Damansara on Saturday evening. Petaling Jaya district police chief Assistant Commissioner Shamsudin Mamat said police received a report at 7.56pm. The victim suffered a fractured eye socket and several other injuries.

items.title: DNB faces three years of losses after share transfer
items.summary: CIMB Securities has forecast cumulative net losses of RM1.5 billion between FY26 and FY28, while warning that the share transfer could result in a half-year earnings impact for each participating telco in FY26. The firm expects DNB to record net losses of RM700 million in FY26, RM500 million in FY27 and RM300 million in FY28. CIMB Securities estimates every one percentage point uplift in mobile revenue growth will help boost CelcomDigi Bhd's and Maxis Bhd's core net profit by three to five per cent.

items.title: Police hunt two men after YouTuber badly hurt in Bandar Sri Damansara attack
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, July 13 — A YouTuber and football commentator suffered serious injuries after being assaulted by two unidentified men at a petrol station near Bandar Sri Damansara here last Saturday. According to Harian Metro (HM), Petaling Jaya district police chief Assistant Commissioner Shamsudin Mamat said his team received a report about the incident at 7.56pm. “The suspects repeatedly assaulted the victim before recording a video of the attack and fleeing the scene,” he said in a statement, adding that the victim sustained a fractured eye socket along with several other injuries.

items.title: Putrajaya lines up RM207m projects to spur Pasir Puteh’s ECRL-linked growth
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 13 — The government has approved 46 projects for the Pasir Puteh parliamentary constituency in Kelantan for 2026, with a total allocation ceiling of RM207.2 million, including downstream industrial infrastructure to support activities near the East Coast Rail Link (ECRL) cargo station. Deputy Economy Minister Datuk Mohd Shahar Abdullah said the approved projects include land preparation and infrastructure development for the Pasir Puteh downstream industrial area, in line with efforts to leverage the ECRL as a catalyst for local economic growth. “The development of the area surrounding the Pasir Puteh ECRL Station is being planned in an integrated manner under the ECRL Integrated Land Use Master Plan (PGTA–ECRL), with the station identified as both a hub for passengers and cargo and logistics.

items.title: Saarani: Politics not behind delay in Perak-Penang water deal
items.summary: IPOH: Perak Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Saarani Mohamad today denied that political factors were behind the delay in signing the bulk water supply agreement between Perak and Penang. He said the agreement was a business-to-business (B2B) arrangement between Khazanah Air Perak (KAP) and Penang's water management company, with no involvement from the state government. It is the party negotiating and signing the agreement with Penang's water management company," he said.

items.title: Zelenskyy orders new PM in major government reshuffle
items.summary: KYIV: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy announced a major reshuffle of the government on Sunday, proposing the replacement of the prime minister and the heads of some law enforcement agencies. Zelenskyy did not say who would replace incumbent Prime Minister Yulia Svyrydenko or elaborate on the reasons for the changes. He also said Ukraine needed to make preparations for winter, following repeated Russian strikes on Ukraine's energy grid each year.

items.title: Delay in Perak–Penang water transfer pact not political, Saarani insists talks remain strictly business
items.summary: IPOH, July 13 — Perak Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Saarani Mohamad today said that the delay in signing the agreement for the Perak-Penang water transfer project was not due to political reasons. Saarani stressed that the matter was purely a business arrangement between Khazanah Air Perak (KAP) and the Penang Water Supply Corporation, and not between the Perak and Penang state governments. Asked when the agreement would be signed, Saarani said the timeline now depends entirely on Penang.

items.title: Nearly 13,000 sit repeat SPM papers from tomorrow
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A total of 12,974 candidates will sit for the 2026 SPM Repeat Examination at 188 examination centres nationwide from July 14 to 16. The Education Ministry (MoE) said the examination would cover three subjects — Bahasa Melayu, History and Mathematics. In a statement today, it said 1,530 examination personnel had been appointed to oversee the written papers and Bahasa Melayu Listening Test, while 1,366 personnel would manage the Bahasa Melayu Speaking Test.

items.title: Petronas to supply LNG to Shizuoka Gas for seven years from 2032
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Petronas LNG Ltd, a subsidiary of Petroliam Nasional Bhd (Petronas), has signed a new liquefied natural gas (LNG) sale and purchase agreement (SPA) with Japan's Shizuoka Gas Co Ltd to supply about 0.84 million tonnes of LNG over seven years from 2032. The agreement extends a partnership spanning three decades and is aimed at strengthening long-term energy security while providing Shizuoka Gas with greater supply flexibility through Petronas' diversified global LNG portfolio. Petronas said it will continue leveraging its integrated global LNG portfolio to provide reliable energy supplies while supporting energy security and a responsible energy transition.

items.title: Malaysia Airlines partners France's SNCF Voyageurs for seamless air-rail travel
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia Airlines has partnered with France's national rail operator SNCF Voyageurs to offer seamless air-rail travel between Paris Charles de Gaulle Airport and 28 destinations across France under a new codeshare agreement. The partnership, offered through SNCF Voyageurs' Train+Air product, allows passengers flying with Malaysia Airlines to book a single itinerary combining flights and high-speed rail services beyond Paris. Under the arrangement, Malaysia Airlines will place its marketing code on SNCF-operated rail services via AccesRail's IATA code "9B", enabling passengers to enjoy coordinated flight and rail connections with a single booking and simplified transfers.

items.title: Don’t panic: Aircraft disaster exercise scheduled for Dash highway on July 16
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, July 13 — Motorists and members of the public are advised that a full-scale aircraft disaster exercise, Latih Amal Ex Urban Falcon 2026, will take place at the Denai Alam Rest and Relax (R&R) area on the Damansara-Shah Alam Elevated Expressway (Dash). Organised by Malaysia Airports in collaboration with the National Disaster Management Agency (Nadma), the Selangor State Secretary, Prolintas-Dash, and the Department of Information Malaysia (JaPen), the drill forms part of the 2026 National Air Disaster Preparedness Programme under the theme "Integrasi Perkasa, Aspirasi Nusa." What to expect Dash Highway users and residents near the Denai Alam R&R may observe the following during the simulation: The presence of emergency vehicles and sounding sirens.

items.title: Beds not enough: Higher ed minister vows 70pc hostel coverage by 2035, prioritising B40 needs
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 13 — Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abd Kadir said the government is targeting to provide residential college facilities for at least 70 per cent of full-time undergraduate students at public universities and polytechnics nationwide by 2035, as part of efforts to address the student accommodation shortage. He said the Ministry of Higher Education (MOHE) developed the Residential College Facilities Policy for Public Higher Education Institutions (IPTA) in November 2024 through cooperation with the Malaysian Universities Housing Council and engagement sessions with student leaders. “Moving forward, to meet students’ accommodation needs, more student residential colleges will be developed as part of MOHE’s long-term plan to ensure their comfort and welfare, particularly for students from the B40 income group,” he told the Dewan Rakyat today.

items.title: ‘It’s not easy to be with me’: Syed Saddiq praises Bella, hints at marriage after acquittal
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 13 — Former Muar MP Syed Saddiq Syed Abdul Rahman expressed a sigh of relief, shed tears with his parents, prayed and then alluded to wedding plans after the Federal Court threw out the prosecution’s appeal against his acquittal of his conviction and sentence over four charges involving alleged misuse of Angkatan Bersatu Anak Muda (Armada) funds. Bella visibly happy was quietly shedding tears on the side while Syed Saddiq spoke to the media. He alluded to wanting to take things to the next level with Bella but said he wouldn’t make the announcement in court.

items.title: ‘Sabah is open for business’: Hajiji courts investors with promise of sustainable growth, carbon‑negative credentials
items.summary: Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor said Sabah was positioning itself as one of the region’s leading destinations for sustainable investment and invited investors to establish long-term partnerships that could deliver measurable social and environmental benefits alongside financial returns. “Sabah’s economic transformation has been remarkable. Sabah is open for business,” he said.

items.title: From RM2 to RM59.6m: Melaka’s tourist levy grows into heritage windfall
items.summary: MELAKA, July 13 — The Melaka government has collected RM59.6 million in heritage charges imposed on out-of-state visitors staying at registered hotels in the state since 2012. State Tourism, Heritage, Arts and Culture Committee chairman Datuk Abdul Razak Abdul Rahman said the Melaka Historic City Council (MBMB) contributed the largest share at RM50.9 million, followed by Hang Tuah Jaya Municipal Council (MPHTJ) with RM6.6 million, Alor Gajah Municipal Council (MPAG) with RM1.8 million, and Jasin Municipal Council (MPJ) with RM209,000. “Of the state government’s 60 per cent share, RM34,668,367.90 has been channelled to the government through the Melaka State Heritage Charge Trust Account.

items.title: Federal Court upholds Syed Saddiq acquittal in RM1m Armada case with split 2–1 ruling
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 13 — The Federal Court today upheld Muar MP Syed Saddiq Syed Abdul Rahman’s acquittal on four criminal charges linked to RM1 million in Armada funds after dismissing the prosecution’s appeal in a 2-1 majority decision. Federal Court judges Datuk Che Mohd Ruzima Ghazali and Datuk Collin Lawrence Sequerah agreed to dismiss the prosecution’s appeal, affirming last year’s Court of Appeal ruling that acquitted the former youth and sports minister. Court of Appeal President Datuk Seri Abu Bakar Jais dissented, finding that the Court of Appeal had erred and that Syed Saddiq should have been convicted on three of the four charges.

items.title: Man’s hunting trip in Sabah forest ends in death, two companions face murder probe as police seize homemade guns
items.summary: SIPITANG, July 13 — Two men in their 50s have been remanded after the body of a man who went missing following a fishing and hunting trip a week earlier was found wrapped in canvas in a forest area near Kampung Takula on Friday. The victim is believed to have been shot during the hunting trip, with his body allegedly abandoned by his two companions. Sipitang police chief Supt Awang Anak Suanda said the body was discovered about 1km from Kampung Takula following information obtained after questioning the two suspects.

items.title: Malaysia renews call to abolish UNSC veto, says abuse undermines global peace and credibility
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 13 — Malaysia has reiterated its longstanding position that the veto power held by the five permanent members of the United Nations Security Council (UNSC) is unjust and should ultimately be abolished, while reaffirming its commitment to championing reforms of the UN system. “Malaysia has consistently supported and called for comprehensive reform of the UNSC, including changes to the veto system. Malaysia’s position is clear, the veto power is unfair and should be abolished.

items.title: When KK City Hall gets tough: One man knocks down own house in Likas squatter colony ahead of bulldozers
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, July 13 — A squatter demolished his own home while authorities tore down 38 other illegal units during an integrated operation at a settlement behind Likas Square on Thursday. It involved 25 personnel from several agencies, including the Kota Kinabalu City Hall (DBKK) Enforcement Department, police, Sabah Electricity, and the Assistant Collector of Land Revenue. In a statement, DBKK stressed that any construction without approval on government land, including river and drainage reserves, is an offence subject to enforcement under existing laws.

items.title: One burnt, one trapped: Night of tragedy on Sarawak roads as two drivers die in crashes
items.summary: SIBU, July 13 — Two separate fatal accidents in Sarawak last night and early this morning claimed the lives of two drivers. One victim was burned to death after her vehicle caught fire, while the other died after being trapped inside his car. Mukah police chief Deputy Supt Muhamad Rizal Alias said the victim was driving from Mukah towards her home in Sungai Pelugau, Selangau, at the time of the incident.

items.title: Scammers go rural: Senator says Sarawak villagers are the new targets to become money mules
items.summary: BINTULU, July 13 — The growing reach of scam syndicates in rural areas is concerning, said Senator Abun Sui Anyit. He warned that vulnerable communities were being exploited through offers of money to open bank and e-wallet accounts for criminal activities. “Syndicates typically offer cash incentives to villagers to open bank accounts or Touch ‘n Go e-wallet accounts under their own names, without informing them that the accounts would later be used for online fraud and money laundering activities,” he said in a statement.

items.title: Don’t fall for it: Kuching school principal says scammers are claiming to be from CHMS No. 4 to cash in on public trust
items.summary: KUCHING, July 13 — Chung Hua Middle School (CHMS) No. 4 has condemned the actions of individuals who allegedly impersonated the school and its principal to solicit funds from the public. 4 principal Johnny Kon said he had received numerous reports from the public stating they had been contacted via text messages and other means asking for financial assistance.

items.title: Man arrested after allegedly torching mother’s house in Jitra following bitter family quarrel
items.summary: According to Berita Harian (BH), Kubang Pasu district police chief Superintendent Mohd Radzi Abdul Rahim said the incident occurred at about 3.15pm when a 28 year old man, who was trading outside the property, saw the suspect enter the house. About 30 minutes later, the suspect emerged and the witness noticed smoke coming from the living room as the suspect left the scene. The witness, who is the suspect’s cousin, later confirmed the house belonged to the suspect’s 60 year old mother, who now lives in Pokok Sena.

items.title: Crowds gather early as Syed Saddiq, family and fiancée await high‑stakes Federal Court ruling
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 13 — Muar Member of Parliament Syed Saddiq Syed Abdul Rahman arrived at the Palace of Justice at 8.50 this morning to learn his fate as to whether he will walk free or be sent to prison. He faces four charges related to criminal breach of trust, misappropriation of property and money laundering involving funds from the youth wing Angkatan Bersatu Anak Muda (Armada). The 33-year-old was accompanied by his parents, Shariffah Mahani Syed Abdul Aziz and Syed Abdul Rahman Abdullah Asagoff, as well as his fiancée, actress Bella Astillah.

items.title: PAS accepts Umno lead, declaring Malay‑Muslim leadership must drive Negeri Sembilan government
items.summary: SEREMBAN, July 13 — PAS is prepared to accept an Umno candidate as Negeri Sembilan menteri besar if cooperation between the two parties can be finalised, its president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang said. We accept the reality that Negeri Sembilan is dominated by Umno. Abdul Hadi said discussions included Umno retaining its 14 seats, while PAS would keep its three.

items.title: One wrong fall: Tree-felling job leaves worker in Sabah with serious head injuries
items.summary: TAMPARULI, July 13 — A 57-year-old man suffered serious head injuries after being struck by a falling tree while carrying out tree-felling work at Jalan Perumahan Kionsom Baru here on Sunday. The Fire and Rescue Department said it received an emergency call at 4.10pm and dispatched seven personnel from the Tuaran Fire and Rescue Station to the scene, about 23km away. The victim was found about 700 metres from the main road, where firefighters provided initial assistance before carrying him out on a stretcher to an awaiting ambulance.

items.title: Tawau dinner drama: Bandar Fajar restaurant fight lands two women in hot water
items.summary: Tawau district police chief Superintendent Wilynton Enchana Watt said both suspects were detained by the district’s Criminal Investigation Division following a police report lodged over the incident. “Acting on information, a team from the Tawau Criminal Investigation Division tracked down and arrested both suspects by the roadside along Jalan Haji Karim. Police said both suspects have no prior criminal records, and no items were seized during the arrests.

items.title: Malaysia rolls out royal welcome for Singapore President Tharman in his first state visit
items.summary: SEPANG, July 13 — Singapore President Tharman Shanmugaratnam arrived in Malaysia last night for a three-day state visit from July 13 to 15 at the invitation of His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia. This is Tharman’s first state visit to Malaysia since assuming office as the ninth President of Singapore on Sept 14, 2023. Total bilateral trade between Malaysia and Singapore reached RM402.35 billion (US$93.97 billion) in 2025, representing an increase of 1.5 per cent compared with 2024.

items.title: Wisma Putra confirms death of Malaysian scaling Europe’s highest peak in Russia
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 13 — A Malaysian died while climbing Mount Elbrus in Russia on Saturday (July 11), the Foreign Ministry confirmed. In a statement on Sunday, the ministry, also known as Wisma Putra, said the deceased was among three Malaysians taking part in a Mount Elbrus climbing expedition organised by a local operator. The ministry also extended its deepest condolences to the family of the deceased.
